errors.landingChat_intro: You are the expert sales assistant for REPUTEXA. You have access to an 'UP-TO-DATE DATA' block below: for any price, percentage or offer detail, you must strictly adhere to it, do not invent figures.
errors.landingChat_keyboardCharter:
KEYBOARD STYLE (MANDATORY for all text you produce):
- No long dashes (em dash), no en dashes. No incises with dashes or " - ": use commas, periods or parentheses.
- Ellipses: three characters ... and never the single glyph "…".
- Straight quotes " and straight apostrophe ' if you use them.
- No non-breaking spaces; normal spaces.
errors.landingChat_groundedFactsCharter:
ACCURACY (MANDATORY):
- Do not invent any fact: no products, brands, sourcing locations, team names, hours, specific 'house' details, or figures or deadlines, unless explicitly stated in the provided context or system instructions.
- If information is missing, state it or remain intentionally general ('according to your feelings', 'as indicated on-site') without fabricating it.
- Never present a supposition as a certainty.
errors.landingChat_roleAndProduct:
Role: to inform accurately about the product, the Vision / Pulse / Zenith plans, the trial, multi-site billing, features (AI responses to reviews, alerts, PDFs, WhatsApp, SEO, ZENITH review collection, etc.). Your tone is professional, reassuring, clear.

Product (summary):
- REPUTEXA = management of e-reputation for local SMEs (restaurants, accommodation, beauty, health, retail...).
- The AI writes responses to reviews, with personalisation (tone, length). Pulse/Zenith: WhatsApp alerts for very negative reviews, advanced reporting, shield / abuse reporting, ZENITH: Maps SEO layer, automated review collection, enhanced AI checks.
- 14-day trial; card to validate access; no charge during the trial period if the offer indicates; cancellation from the customer area.

Rules:
- Never promise 'guaranteed removal' of a review: talk about reporting procedures to platforms, proactive management and compliance.
- If a question exceeds your knowledge base, say so honestly and refer to /pricing or the trial signup.
errors.landingChat_liveFacts:
=== UP-TO-DATE DATA (synchronised with config/pricing.ts, use these figures for any pricing question) ===
First establishment, monthly GBP: Vision {visionGbp}£, Pulse {pulseGbp}£, Zenith {zenithGbp}£.
Second, third… establishment: tiered discounts on Stripe, approximately -20%, -30%, -40%, then -50% on subsequent ones.
Annual billing: -20% on the total (display type 'equivalent / month' on the site).
Trial: 14 days on plans; card to validate access; simple cancellation from the customer area.
Pages: /pricing (offer details), /signup?mode=trial (trial).
errors.landingChat_leadCaptureInstruction: The user has asked several questions. Naturally propose: 'I can assist you further. What is the name of your establishment? I will conduct a quick audit of your e-reputation and send you personalised recommendations.' Encourage engagement without being pushy.

PricingPage.headline: Select Your Plan
PricingPage.subtitle: Free Trial · No Commitment
PricingPage.paymentCancelledToast: Payment not completed. Your options have been retained.
PricingPage.resumeCheckoutToast: You had started a subscription. Would you like to continue?
PricingPage.stripeReassurance: Payment secured by Stripe. No commitment.
PricingPage.trialMention: £0 today. Card required to validate access. Cancel with one click from your customer area.
PricingPage.prorataMessage: Prorata is calculated automatically. You only pay the difference at the time of the change.
PricingPage.zenithDataConsent: I confirm that my customers are informed of the use of their contact details for review solicitation (WhatsApp), in accordance with GDPR.
PricingPage.legalCheckHint: Tick this box to activate payment.
PricingPage.planBlockedZenith: Accept the terms and customer confirmation to continue.
PricingPage.visionDesc: Gateway: save time and maintain a clean Google profile, without the toolkit of higher plans.
PricingPage.visionFeature1: Automated, truly personalised review responses: AI reads every sentence and responds to the customer's words (e.g. 'thank you for the complimentary coffee' → the response mentions this gesture). Strict formality, echoing customer phrasing, professional finish.
PricingPage.visionFeature2: Monthly PDF reporting by email: health report of a few pages — volume, average rating, trends. Factual findings, not the 'consultancy level' of higher plans.
PricingPage.visionFeature3: Mastered human tone: no one should think of a machine — polished syntax, just the right amount of emotion, never excessive.
PricingPage.visionFeature4: One language per account: that of your market (e.g. Spanish company → responses in natural Spanish). Negative reviews do not go out via WhatsApp: they arrive in the Alerts tab of the dashboard with a modifiable AI suggestion, then a publication queue (human delay), like other responses.
PricingPage.visionFeature5: Vision scope: no real-time WhatsApp alerts, no automated customer collection, no advanced SEO boost or triple engine — upgrade to Pulse or Zenith for these features.
PricingPage.pulseDesc: Peace of mind: active monitoring, difficult reviews on WhatsApp, reports that tell you what to do.
PricingPage.pulseFeature1: Same writing requirements as Vision: line-by-line analysis, customer words echoed, formality, zero generic messages.
PricingPage.pulseFeature2: 1–2★ review alerts on WhatsApp (~30 s): you see the review, the proposed response; modify by text or voice, then Publish → enters the queue with a realistic publication timer.
PricingPage.pulseFeature3: Monthly 'consultant' PDF: beyond the numbers — comparison with previous months, recurring themes, concrete action points like a strategist (predictive sentiment/internal audit when the data allows).
PricingPage.pulseFeature4: Weekly WhatsApp recap (Monday 8 am UTC): clear figures, what’s going well / what’s not, direct tone like a consultant — plus detailed weekly history in Statistics.
PricingPage.pulseFeature5: Toxic review shield: alert in the dashboard + structured reporting file (AI) to review and then submit; threatening/abusive reviews may also trigger an immediate WhatsApp with a link to Alerts.
PricingPage.pulseFeature6: AI indistinguishable from a human in form: not too 'robotically polite', just enough.
PricingPage.pulseFeature7: Triple internal draft + selection of the best response (social psychology criteria) before sending to the queue.
PricingPage.pulseFeature8: Native multilingual responses (not Google Translate) for tourists and international clientele.
PricingPage.zenithDesc: Marketing Director 24/7: everything Pulse offers, plus SEO Maps, review capture, and triple AI safeguards.
PricingPage.zenithFeature1: Review responses: same quality promise — fine reading, customer echo, formality, anti-repetition control.
PricingPage.zenithFeature2: Triple AI Verification (three different logics + judge): the most convincing response enters the queue.
PricingPage.zenithFeature3: Negative review alerts on WhatsApp + modifiable suggestion + publication queue, like Pulse.
PricingPage.zenithFeature4: Enhanced tactical/strategic monthly PDF + link with your weekly recaps for a vision that matures over time.
PricingPage.zenithFeature5: Weekly WhatsApp recap + archives by week in Statistics (premises filter).
PricingPage.zenithFeature6: SEO Maps boost: weak keywords detected, naturally injected into responses and WhatsApp solicitations (capture).
PricingPage.zenithFeature7: Same toxic review shield as Pulse: prepared file in Alerts + immediate WhatsApp on serious threats.
PricingPage.zenithFeature8: Automated review collection via WhatsApp (checkout/webhook), clear consent, human tone — as already planned in REPUTEXA.
PricingPage.zenithFeature10: Native multilingual support across all customer-facing elements.
PricingPage.visionDetailsExtended:
What you get concretely with Vision:

• Responses generated by premium AI models (like GPT/Claude depending on our stack), with anti-'robot' instructions: variable structure from one response to another, bouncing off the customer's exact phrasing, formality, zero generic copy-pasting.

• Reviews of 3 stars and above generally follow the automatic flow: the response is prepared immediately, then published after a few hours' queue (the 'human response time' effect), with a visible countdown. As long as it is in the queue, you can still modify the text.

• More severe reviews (less than 3 stars) do not go out via WhatsApp: they arrive in the Alerts tab of the dashboard, with an AI suggestion that you validate or rewrite before queuing for publication — as planned for this plan.

• Monthly PDF by email: factual health report (volume, average rating, trend) over a few pages. This is not the same 'consultancy/predictive level' as on Pulse or Zenith.

• One primary language aligned with your market. Multi-premises: each site has its own views (reviews, stats, alerts); when managing several, some sends (e.g. reports) can also be thought of globally.

What is not included in Vision: real-time WhatsApp alerts on negative reviews, automated customer collection via WhatsApp, advanced SEO boost on Maps, triple cross-verification between multiple AI logics — reserved for Pulse and Zenith plans. After subscription, everything related to tone, length of responses, and your personal instructions remains adjustable in Settings.
PricingPage.pulseDetailsExtended:
Pulse retains the Vision promise and adds active monitoring:

• Same writing requirement (line-by-line reading, customer words echoed, credible human tone). On the engine side: multiple drafts are compared internally, and only the most convincing response goes to your publication queue.

• 1 or 2-star reviews trigger a WhatsApp alert in very little time: you see the review, the proposed response, you can modify it (text or voice), then validate publication — which goes back through the queue with a realistic delay, like other reviews.

• Monthly 'consultant' PDF: beyond raw numbers — comparison with previous months, recurring themes, action points, and lines like audit/trends when the data supports analysis.

• Every Monday at 8 am (UTC): WhatsApp recap (direct tone, figures, what’s going well / what’s blocking) and, in the same motion, a more complete version archived in Statistics, navigable week by week.

• Abusive or clearly rule-breaking reviews: a prepared file in Alerts for review and reporting; serious cases can also trigger an immediate WhatsApp with a link to the Alerts page.

• Responses in multiple languages, treated as native (not just a simple automatic translation). Multi-premises: selector at the top of the dashboard, sealed data by site for the most part; recaps can focus on the most critical premises while maintaining an overall view.
PricingPage.zenithDetailsExtended:
Zenith includes everything in Pulse, with the 'marketing & SEO' layer:

• Review responses: same quality charter, plus a triple verification protocol (multiple AI 'voices' that will challenge the chosen response) to minimise errors or awkwardness before sending to the queue.

• SEO Google Maps boost: identification of intentions/keywords where you are weak, then natural integration into responses and WhatsApp review solicitation messages — without visible advertising blocks.

• Automated review collection via WhatsApp after payment (checkout, webhook, etc.), with explicit consent and human tone — as already planned in the REPUTEXA ecosystem.

• Negative review alerts on WhatsApp, editable suggestions, toxic files in Alerts: same principles as Pulse, with an additional layer of tools for multi-site groups.

• Monthly PDF reports and weekly recaps designed to link short-term (week's action) and medium-term (month's priorities). Multiple premises: internal rankings, comparisons, and deliverables tailored in the spirit of 'holding'.

• Publication queue, timers, and the ability to edit until it’s sent: identical to other plans, to maintain a coherent image on the customer side. Tone, length, and AI personal instructions are adjustable in Settings; each location can be refined over time.
PricingPage.compareSubtitle: Vision, Pulse, and Zenith: same definitions as on the pricing page, presented side by side for a comprehensive view.
PricingPage.compareModalHint: ✓ included in the plan · ✗ not included in this plan. Contractual detail: T&Cs and Pricing page.
PricingPage.tooltipCompareAll: Ouvre un tableau détaillé : Vision, Pulse et Zénith côte à côte.
PricingPage.tooltipBillingCycle: Basculer entre paiement au mois et paiement à l’année. Le badge indique l’économie annuelle.
PricingPage.tooltipBadgePulse: Le plus choisi : alertes temps réel, récap hebdo et réponses IA de haute qualité.
PricingPage.tooltipBadgeZenith: Offre complète : tout Pulse plus SEO, collecte d’avis et triple vérification IA.
PricingPage.compareMatrixFootnote: ✓ / ✗ = included or not in the product offering. For the definitive legal framework (Zenith, end customers, durations), see the T&Cs and privacy policy.
PricingPage.matrixRow_aiPersonalized_detail: Each text starts from the actual content of the review — no stock phrases. Goal: a professional, credible, and human tone, with anti-repetition instructions to avoid the 'robot' effect.
PricingPage.matrixRow_humanQueue_detail: The response does not go out immediately: a realistic delay enhances credibility. Until the moment of publication, you can still modify the text in the dashboard.
PricingPage.matrixRow_singleLanguage: Vision: One Primary Language for the Entire Account
PricingPage.matrixRow_singleLanguage_detail: Suitable for a stable clientele in one language (e.g. establishment in France → responses in French). This is not the advanced 'native' multilingual support of Pulse and Zenith plans.
PricingPage.matrixRow_multilingual_detail: The review is detected in its language; the response is written in that same language, naturally — useful for tourists, cross-border clients, or reviews in English/Spanish/etc.
PricingPage.matrixRow_tripleDraft: Multiple Internal Drafts, One Response Retained
PricingPage.matrixRow_tripleDraft_detail: AI generates and compares multiple versions; only the strongest (clarity, empathy, coherence with the review) is proposed to you and then queued, to limit weak responses.
PricingPage.matrixRow_tripleJudge: Zenith: Triple Verification Before Sending to Queue
PricingPage.matrixRow_tripleJudge_detail: Three different approaches analyse the response, then a 'judge' layer decides: the objective is to reduce awkwardness, inaccuracies, or risky formulations before publication.
PricingPage.matrixRow_pdfBasic_detail: Volume of reviews, average rating, trend: the essentials for tracking your e-reputation without PowerPoint slide jargon.
PricingPage.matrixRow_pdfConsultant_detail: Beyond the numbers: recurring themes, inter-month comparisons, concrete action points; sometimes 'audit' or trend readings when the data allows.
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappBad: Very Negative Reviews (1–2★): WhatsApp Alert + Draft
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappBad_detail: You quickly receive the review and a proposed response on WhatsApp, modifiable before publication. In Vision, these reviews go into Alerts without real-time WhatsApp alerts.
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappWeekly: Weekly WhatsApp Recap + History in Statistics
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappWeekly_detail: Every week: summary of figures and notable topics; a detailed version is archived for retrieving week-by-week history.
PricingPage.matrixRow_toxicShield: Toxic or Threatening Reviews: File & Reporting Assistance
PricingPage.matrixRow_toxicShield_detail: Structured file in the dashboard to prepare a request to Google with arguments for review; serious cases can also trigger an immediate WhatsApp alert.
PricingPage.matrixRow_mapsSeo: Zenith: SEO Boost for Google Maps
PricingPage.matrixRow_mapsSeo_detail: Identifies intentions or expressions where you are weak and suggests integrating them naturally into your public responses and, if necessary, in solicitation messages — without stuffing or aggressive advertising tone.
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappCapture: Zenith: Review Collection via WhatsApp After Purchase
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappCapture_detail: After payment (checkout, webhook, etc.), a thread may invite feedback or reviews according to your settings and displayed legal mentions. REPUTEXA is the technical tool; you remain responsible for informing your end customers (GDPR).
PricingPage.matrixRow_whatsappSampling_detail: WhatsApp review invitations are not sent to every eligible customer: the platform uses controlled sampling (by default about one in ten eligible checkouts, configurable) to cap volume, avoid a spammy perception, and keep the channel credible. Public Google replies echo the review wording per dashboard tone and length settings.
PricingPage.matrixRow_cooldown120_detail: Same number, same merchant account: no new solicitation campaign before 120 days after the start of the previous one — the platform blocks below this. variants.review: Adorable welcome, buttery croissants just like in the countryside. The birthday order with a name on the brioche: a flawless execution. My mother said it was her best galette in ten years.
variants.response: Thank you Camille for this feedback that makes the whole team shine. We are delighted that the galette and the birthday order have fulfilled you: this is exactly the artisanal level we strive to maintain daily.

variants.review: Breathtaking view at breakfast, firm bedding just how we like it, and the local gift box in the room was a rare touch. The staff took the time to explain the hikes — we recommend it to slow travel lovers.
variants.response: Thank you so much Thomas and Léa: reading that the bedding and panoramic breakfast enhanced your stay motivates us daily. We are also pleased that the hiking tips pleased you; we look forward to welcoming you for another season by the lake.
variants.seoContext: hotel lake view comfort slow travel

variants.review: Complete service + tyres before winter: everything was shown to me, clear invoice without surprises. They spotted a disc play before I broke down on the A7. Professional and human service, rare at a garage.
variants.response: Thank you Karim for this trust: showing the parts and anticipating the disc play is part of our 'zero bad surprise' philosophy. See you soon for the next service or a quick check.

variants.review: Completely botched cut when I showed three photos + video; the shade has nothing to do with it. I was charged for a keratin treatment without being informed of the price. I leave humiliated and angry.
variants.response: Madam M., we are sincerely sorry: such a stark deviation from your inspiration and a service charged without prior agreement is unacceptable. We propose to reschedule with our artistic director to correct or refund according to your preference.

variants.review: Steak arrived lukewarm, soggy fries and wine list 'unavailable' on the first three choices. The waitress signalled that she was overwhelmed but zero apology from the house before the bill.
variants.response: Mr L., we sincerely apologise: a cold meat and disorganised service are not our standard. We would like to invite you back for a service monitored by the floor manager; please contact us privately to arrange a date.

variants.review: Parcel promised before the holidays, arrived 11 days late with damaged packaging. Customer service took 4 days to respond and sent me back to a generic form despite it being a baby gift.

variants.review: Complete scammers, your chef is a nobody and your waiters are incompetent. I wish you bankruptcy, bunch of thieves.

variants.review: Worst night of my life, your hotel is a rat pit run by cowards. If I see you, I will explain in person.

0.review: Completely botched cut! I showed a precise photo and the result has nothing to do with it. Plus, I was charged for a treatment I didn't ask for. I'm really upset.

problem.headline: The Silent Threat: Unmanaged Customer Feedback.
problem.intro: Every day, restaurateurs lose hours managing reviews, suffer devastating 1-star ratings, and fail to amplify positive feedback. One bad review can destroy your Google rating—and your bookings.
problem.solution: Introducing your 24/7 Customer Relations Director. An AI that never sleeps, protects your reputation, and transforms satisfied customers into ambassadors.

negativeFilter.headline: The Ultimate Shield: How We Eradicate 1-Star Reviews.
negativeFilter.step1: The AI contacts the customer via WhatsApp: 'How was your experience?'
negativeFilter.step2: If negative, the AI responds: 'We are sorry. Rather than publishing on Google, would you prefer to contact the owner privately to resolve this?'
negativeFilter.step3: The customer expresses privately. The owner receives a discreet WhatsApp alert.
negativeFilter.result: The 1-star review never appears on Google. This feature alone is worth more than the subscription.

tunnel.title: The REPUTEXA conversation flow
tunnel.subtitle: Goal: improve service with honest feedback — not a blunt review ask. When the customer confirms, AI prepares paste-ready text in the clipboard; one button opens the Google or TripAdvisor listing: they paste, pick their star rating, and publish — no retyping, and they always have the final say.

1.title: First touch (the right moment)
1.body: After purchase, on the delay you choose, a short warm message asks for two minutes to share what worked or what could be better — yes or no, clearly. If they decline or send STOP: a simple thank-you, no more follow-ups on this topic, and they are blocklisted for this flow (duration per your settings, often 120 days or permanent).

2.title: Receipt context → human question
2.body: If they say yes, the chat uses what we know from the visit — ticket, service, package — to invite a few words on what landed or what felt off: conversational tone, not a survey.

3.title: Paste-ready review text
3.body: Once feedback is in, AI suggests a short line that keeps their wording and a believable local touch — already in the clipboard. They can still edit however they want (typing, voice, photos) until they are happy; every tweak is taken into account.

4.title: Button → review page, paste, stars
4.body: After they confirm the draft, the business does not send one long message: first a short reminder to copy the draft (long-press the message above), then a separate message with only the Google write-review link. In production, a smart link can also copy the text automatically and then open Google (intermediate page). They paste, pick their star rating, and publish.

5.title: A sincere thank-you
5.body: A final clear message: thanks for their time, reminder that contact details will not be reused for this outreach, and that data tied to this request is processed until deletion within the window shown at the point of sale — often within 120 days in practice.

tunnel.demoError: Unable to generate the demo at this time.

tunnel.operationalNote: In production, WhatsApp outreach is not sent to every customer: a controlled sample (by default about 1 in 10 eligible visits, per your settings) limits noise and saturation. The Google preview shows a public reply that echoes the reviewer’s words, matching dashboard tone, length, and instructions like in real use.

testimonials.headline: What professionals around the world are saying
testimonials.subtitle: +2,400 establishments manage their reputation with REPUTEXA.
testimonials.highlightCountLabel: {count, plural, one {# client testimonial} other {# client testimonials}}

sophie.quote: The intelligent filter alone has saved us. We had 2-3 unjust reviews per month that were dragging us down. Now? Zero public catastrophes. It's worth every penny for peace of mind.
sophie.name: Sophie M.

marco.quote: It's like having a reputation director 24/7. I receive WhatsApp alerts for emergencies, and the AI manages the rest. Our rating went from 3.9 to 4.8 in 4 months. Unmissable.
marco.name: Marco D.

isabelle.quote: Serenity is what I pay for. The ZENITH plan is the best investment we've made: review collection and AI responses run effortlessly and our Google visibility has exploded.
isabelle.name: Isabelle R.

matteo.quote: Finally an AI that understands the rhythm of an Italian restaurant: reviews come in during service, and I no longer have to choose between the dining room and Google. In a few weeks the rating rises and the comments better describe what really pleases. Even on a busy Saturday, the responses remain worthy of the house. I can finally sleep without monitoring the stars at 2 am.
matteo.name: Matteo G.

yuki.quote: In Kyoto, we welcome travellers from all over the world; on Google, expectations vary according to cultures. WhatsApp alerts signal the urgent, and responses respect our codes of kindness without sounding cold. Our clients see the same requirement online as in the ryokan. It has become a team reflex, not an administrative chore.
yuki.name: Yuki T.

james.quote: Pub in London: the Maps rating went from 3.8 to 4.7, and recent reviews finally tell the story of our cuisine, not just the area. Regulars return and tourists find us without passing by. The AI keeps an authentic pub tone, not a corporate speech. Frankly, we would have taken quarters to achieve this consistency manually.
james.name: James W.

lukas.quote: In Bavaria, word of mouth remains king, but before a reservation, people read Google like a second opinion. REPUTEXA saves us hours each week while maintaining a warm house tone. Responses evoke our seasonal dishes and our actual hours without errors. Today the listing reflects the welcome we give in the dining room.
lukas.name: Lukas M.

elena.quote: Busy season in Andalusia: while I coordinate service in the kitchen, the AI responds to each review with the detail I wouldn't have time to write between services. Travellers see that we truly read what they liked or regretted. Our rating has stabilised without sounding artificial. Essential when the terrace is fully booked four nights a week.
elena.name: Elena R.

thiago.quote: In Rio, Google reviews weigh as much as a friend's recommendation even before the client calls. Our churrascaria has doubled bookings in three months because every recent feedback receives a quick and warm response. The tool keeps our Brazilian voice, not a generic model. The team in the dining room focuses on service while the online reputation follows.
thiago.name: Thiago S.

chen.quote: For our hotel in Shanghai, international travellers compare several listings long before booking. Quick, consistent responses in the right tone have transformed how we are perceived outside of China. We can address remarks in English or Chinese without unrealistic delays. It has become a conversion lever as important as the photos.
chen.name: Chen Wei

sarah.quote: Quick service in Texas: our customers expect warmth and spontaneity, not corporate boilerplate. AI responses maintain the tone of our diner, even when Sunday brunch is full. Every review receives a response that shows we really read it. Today I no longer juggle between the grill and Google alerts on my phone.
sarah.name: Sarah J.

googleMaps.q: Can the AI really help me rank higher on Google?
googleMaps.a: Yes. Google (the search engine and maps) favours establishments that respond quickly and accurately. Our AI ensures 24/7 responsiveness. For the ZENITH plan, it strategically injects SEO keywords into your responses to signal to the algorithm that you are the reference in your sector. The more you respond intelligently, the more Google promotes you over your competitors.

iaDetectable.q: Will my customers realise it's AI?
iaDetectable.a: Absolutely not. Our models are trained on thousands of real merchant responses. The tone is warm, professional, and indistinguishable from a human response. For the ZENITH plan, the 'Triple Verification' compares three response variants and only publishes the best. Your customers will feel listened to by a human, with the added time savings.

installTime.q: How long does it take to install?
installTime.a: Less than 2 minutes. You connect your Google Business account, and the AI starts analysing your reviews immediately. There is no software to install; everything happens on your REPUTEXA dashboard or directly on WhatsApp for alerts.

gratuit14j.q: Is it really free for 14 days?
gratuit14j.a: Yes, completely. You enjoy the power of REPUTEXA without spending a penny for two weeks. Although we use Stripe to secure access, no amount is charged before the end of your trial period. You can cancel with one click if you are not impressed by the results. It’s an opportunity to test the impact on your reputation with no risk.

adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_ghost_shield: Ghost Shield / emergency stop: desired state for production, documented if active
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_nexus_slack: Nexus · Support: every opened case is read, resolved or routed (nothing 'sleeps')
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_security_audit: Security: review last audit summary and fixes (paper or backlog)
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_blackbox_sample: Black Box: test search + restore on a recent real extract
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_ia_queue: IA Forge: RLHF queue / human feedback — sorted according to your policy
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_guardian_stats: Code Guardian: debt, last export, trend — you prioritise follow-ups
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_dpia_review: DPIA / transfers / registers: monthly review with compliance referent
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_subprocessors_csv: Subcontractors: CSV / up-to-date list, signed DPA and noted dates
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_investor_archive: Investors: metrics / report — close or archive the period
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_legal_pages: Public legal pages: aligned with supplier & processing realities
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_o_full_export: Sensitive audit exports — deposited in private space, never in Git
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_o_drill_emergency: Emergency stop drill: who calls whom, where is the written procedure
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_o_access_review: Access: review admin accounts, keys, Slack, hosts — least privilege
adminComplianceKit.itemHint_m_legal_pages: → Legal pages section of this file
adminComplianceKit.operatorTraceIntro: Timestamped history (checks, unchecks, resets, archives). Kept in base with the file.
adminComplianceKit.operatorArchiveNotePlaceholder: Optional note (e.g.: end of sprint, before CNIL audit, after incident…)
adminComplianceKit.operatorArchiveHelp: Records the instant T of checked boxes + your note. Useful for proving regular human review.
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_hub_pulse: Admin home: browse modules, stats, and absence of red alert (2–5 min)
adminComplianceKit.itemHint_d_hub_pulse: Overview — you, not just the automations.
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_clients_fire: Clients: list queue — priority 100, security alerts and exceptions seen today
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_d_legal_guardian_inbox: Legal: Guardian drafts / publications — nothing blocking without your decision
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_sentinel_compliance: Sentinel / production health: flash 360 or maintenance panel reviewed
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_w_council_digest: Digest 'agent council': read the latest cron summary (understanding, not execution)
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_vault_sentinel: Sentinel vault / backups: last run OK, escalate if failure
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_m_billing_stripe: Billing: human review of overdue, churn, volumes (Stripe + base consistency)
adminComplianceKit.operatorItem_o_external_audit_walk: External auditor walk-through 20–30 min: read-only navigation, no intervention
adminComplianceKit.itemHint_o_external_audit_walk: → This file + admin home as guest if possible
adminComplianceKit.sectionDocHabitsTitle: Guide habits — files & proofs (excluding admin boxes)
adminComplianceKit.sectionDocHabitsIntro: These boxes help you not forget about public documents and Drive. They remain on this device (browser); the operator file below, however, is saved in base.
adminComplianceKit.habitDocPeriodWeekly: Every week (30–45 min total, spread out)
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_d_readme: Open the README of the kit 5 minutes
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_d_readme: Know where the checklist, exports, sensitive locations not to commit are.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_d_legal_glance: Test the 3 legal pages of the site
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_d_legal_glance: Privacy, terms, mentions — blank page or error = to correct immediately.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_w_guide: Browse a section of the complete verification guide
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_w_guide: Not all at once: one section per week (frequencies, limits of perfect percentage, inventory).
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_w_fortresse: GDPR, work, B2B, tech — one pillar per week to keep the vision.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_m_csv: Update on Drive the subcontractor list + DPA dates
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_m_csv: The CSV template from the kit is the base; the legal truth lives outside Git.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_m_registre: Update the Art. 30 register (CSV or HTML→PDF) and archive the dated version
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_m_registre: One line or section per real processing; countries, purposes, subcontractors aligned.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_m_export_sample: Make an audit export (JSON or CSV) and store it in the right private place
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_m_export_sample: Even for testing: check that you know where the file should live and who accesses it.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_b_law: Open legal question? It is tracked with the counsel or DPO
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_b_law: The app organizes; it does not decide for you.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_b_dpa: Every critical subcontractor has an up-to-date DPA in the internal file
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_b_dpa: Link to the public policy does not replace a signed contract.
adminComplianceKit.habitDoc_b_human_trace: At least one 'human' archive this month (operator file or export)
adminComplianceKit.habitDocHint_b_human_trace: Simple proof that a flesh-and-blood person supervised.
adminComplianceKit.habitDocResetMonthly: Clear the month boxes + human reminder (browser)
adminComplianceKit.habitDocLocalNote: Local storage: another computer or cleared cache = boxes reset.
adminComplianceKit.sectionBeyondAppIntro: The compliance kit page is a framework. Maturity comes from the real work behind.
adminComplianceKit.beyondBulletLaw: Legal advice and positioning (DPIA, transfers, countries, clauses): competent human.
adminComplianceKit.beyondBulletDpa: Content of private files (Drive, signed DPAs, proofs outside code).
adminComplianceKit.beyondBulletDrive: Regularity: without the habit of checking, archiving, and exporting, the tool remains a pretty shell.
adminComplianceKit.beyondBulletHabit: Agents do a lot; the final signature of risks remains with you.
adminComplianceKit.kitFilesCadenceIntro: Each line indicates the recommended frequency. These are not legal obligations: they are markers to not forget. The detail (§7–§12) is in the complete guide.
adminComplianceKit.kitPageRhythmTitle: Summary — the whole page in rhythm
adminComplianceKit.kitPageRhythmDaily: Every day: 'habit guide' boxes (browser) + operator file 'day'; open the 3 legal pages; 5 min on the README of the kit.
adminComplianceKit.kitPageRhythmWeekly: Every week: one section of the complete guide + one Fortress pillar; operator file 'week'.
adminComplianceKit.kitPageRhythmMonthly: Every month: subcontractor CSV (Drive), HTML register→dated PDF, audit export stored outside Git, 'human' archive (file or export); operator file 'month'; compliance folder PDF archived automatically on the 1st (previous month, UTC) or manually from the kit.
adminComplianceKit.kitPageRhythmEvent: At each event: new provider, legal publication, PII migration, incident — open the complete guide §7.1 and follow the mini-checklist.
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqReadme: Daily (~5 min) · also before audit or after long break
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqGuide: No reading in one block: 1 section/week OR at each major change OR checklist §8 before control
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqFortresse: Weekly: 1 pillar in rotation (4 weeks = complete cycle)
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqCsv: At each new subcontractor / DPA · monthly list review · quarterly DPA links
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqRegistreHtml: At each modified processing · dated PDF archived (Drive) at least in monthly review / before audit
adminComplianceKit.kitFileFreqRegistreCsv: Identical to HTML (same info, spreadsheet format for DPO)
adminComplianceKit.sectionLegalCadence: Daily: test the 3 URLs (error = immediate correction). After legal publication admin: recheck display.
adminComplianceKit.sectionAuditExportsCadence: Monthly: a test export + documented private location. Before control or merchant file: targeted exports.
adminComplianceKit.sectionSensitiveCadence: Once at startup, then at each change of tool or log URL; up-to-date copy on Drive (do not commit sensitive URLs).
adminComplianceKit.auditJsonCadence: Same rhythm as the section (monthly / before control / merchant file). 120-day window. Single file: review_queue + consent_logs — to be stored outside Git.
adminComplianceKit.auditCsvQueueCadence: Same calendar. 'Queue' table in Excel; message text column only if the sensitive box is checked (JSON + this CSV).
adminComplianceKit.auditCsvConsentCadence: Same calendar. WhatsApp consent logs (consent/refusal/STOP, hash) — separate file, without message bodies.
adminComplianceKit.bundlePdfIntro: A single PDF: subcontractors, logs template, register (text derived from HTML), journal and frozen archives of the operator file for the chosen month (UTC), state of boxes at generation, signature. Download without archiving, or 'Archive to vault' for the same file in Storage. The cron on the 1st of the month automatically archives the previous civil month (UTC). Store on Drive if you take out a copy — not in Git.
adminComplianceKit.bundlePdfHint: Recommended rhythm: 1st of the month or after the monthly review. The 'perfect' version of the register remains the print from the browser on the HTML URL if an auditor requires the original layout.
adminComplianceKit.bundleArchivesEmpty: No archive yet — after the 1st cron of the month or a manual archiving, files will appear here.
adminComplianceKit.archivesLoadError: Unable to load the archive list.
adminComplianceKit.bundleCronAutoHint: Every 1st of the month (Vercel cron, same secret as the others), the previous UTC month is generated and deposited here if an auto archive does not already exist for this period.
adminComplianceKit.bundleArchiveDone: Archived to admin vault — list updated.

defiReputexa.title: REPUTEXA Challenge
defiReputexa.introObjective: Over a period of your choosing, the team motivates clients to leave glowing reviews — ideally mentioning a name when someone made a difference. Everyone sees their impact on the leaderboard; you set the rewards to encourage full engagement.
defiReputexa.introMechanics: In practice: a window of 1 week to 1 month (often 1 to 2 times a year), a bonus paragraph in WhatsApp during the challenge, points only for the names you track, a live individual leaderboard. The prizes and tiers: you write them yourself; REPUTEXA calculates the points and displays the table.
defiReputexa.rulesSubtitle: A review only counts if a name from your list appears in the comment (or resembles the displayed name of the reviewer — approximation, no legal value). Otherwise: 0 points.
defiReputexa.rule5Text: Tracked name spotted in the review → three points for that person.
defiReputexa.rule4Text: Same logic: one point for the mentioned person.
defiReputexa.ruleLowText: Tracked name spotted in a very negative review → minus one point for that person (if no tracked name matches, nothing happens).
defiReputexa.fieldCompetitionMessage: Text added to WhatsApp messages (active period)
defiReputexa.fieldCompetitionPlaceholder: E.g.: Small internal animation — if someone particularly impressed you, you can mention their name…
defiReputexa.fieldCompetitionHint: This block is added after the usual message as long as the box is checked, dates are filled in, and we are within the window.
defiReputexa.fieldRewards: Rewards (reminder after the end of the challenge)
defiReputexa.fieldRewardsPlaceholder: E.g.: 1st: €100 gift voucher · 2nd: team meal · 3rd: half-day off…
defiReputexa.fieldNamesHint: The score looks for these strings in the review text and the displayed name of the reviewer (approximation — no legal proof).
defiReputexa.leaderboardSubtitle: Reviews imported between the challenge dates. Only the names you have recorded below are taken into account.
defiReputexa.totalPointsHint: Sum of all personal points over the period.
defiReputexa.leaderboardEmpty: No tracked employee for this scoring (add names and save).
defiReputexa.rewardsAttributionTitle: Final Ranking and Reminder of Rewards
defiReputexa.rewardsAttributionIntro: Once the end date has passed, the table below reminds everyone’s place and points obtained. The 'your rewards' block repeats verbatim what you wrote: you alone decide on their implementation with the team.
defiReputexa.rewardsMissingNote: You have not provided a rewards text — remember to keep track internally of what each place receives.
defiReputexa.rewardsDisclaimer: The actual allocation of prizes is solely the responsibility of your business. REPUTEXA displays the ranking and your text as a reminder.
defiReputexa.employeeReviewsSubtitle: Highlighted excerpts of the tracked name spotted in the comment (approximation, no legal value).
defiReputexa.employeeReviewsEmpty: No reviews yet counting points for this person during the challenge period.
defiReputexa.emptyTracked: Add and save tracked names to display the ranking and details by review.
defiReputexa.liveBoardHint: Updated when a new review is imported. Flash cards green or pink during a points change.
defiReputexa.archiveHint: When the end date has passed, archive to keep the ranking below and reset the form for the next challenge.
defiReputexa.archiveOk: Challenge archived. You can start a new one.
defiReputexa.archiveError: Archiving impossible (check that the end date has passed).
defiReputexa.archivesSubtitle: From most recent to oldest — same scoring and summary saved at the time of archiving.
defiReputexa.topHeroIntro: Overview of the tunnel your clients receive: the idea is primarily to improve service thanks to their feedback — not to demand a review upfront. When the challenge period is active (box checked + dates), the block you write below also adds to the sends and free expression — gentle tone, without insistence.
defiReputexa.topHeroStep1: Step 1 — After the chosen delay: message focused on service improvement; explicit yes/no request to grant ~2 minutes. STOP at any time.
defiReputexa.topHeroStep2: Step 2 — The client shares their experience (text or voice). If the challenge is active: optional paragraph on the ongoing animation and the possibility to mention a name, only if they wish.
defiReputexa.topHeroStep3: Step 3 — The AI can propose natural text using **their words** (discreet SEO). The client has the final say: unlimited edits by keyboard, voice, or photos until satisfaction.
defiReputexa.topHeroStep4: Step 4 — Copy to clipboard + link to publish on Google in two clicks.
defiReputexa.topHeroStep5: Step 5 — Thank you and compliance reminder (as on your poster / your commitments).
defiReputexa.topHeroStop: STOP: immediate stop with polite closure message.
defiReputexa.teamLinkIntro: Without connection to the dashboard: paste this link in your group for everyone to see the updated ranking.
defiReputexa.teamLinkScope: Display: reviews from the main profile during the challenge period (secondary establishments: coming soon).
defiReputexa.teamLinkPending: Save the challenge once to activate the permanent link.
defiReputexa.fieldActiveHint: The box remains checked after Save. The bonus 'challenge' messages only go out during the date window; at the end, everything automatically reverts to the standard journey until the next challenge.
defiReputexa.scheduledHint: The dates have not yet started. From the first day, automatic sends will include the challenge block (if you have written it).
defiReputexa.endedBannerBody: The ranking below is frozen on these dates. Archive whenever you want to freeze the recap and prepare for the next challenge, or uncheck 'Activate' to cut the challenge mode without archiving.
defiReputexa.journeyCompareTitle: What does the WhatsApp journey look like?
defiReputexa.journeyCompareSubtitle: On the left: normal flow. On the right: what adds when the challenge is active and we are within your dates (gentle tone, never insistent).
defiReputexa.journeyStep1Delay: About the delay you choose after purchase (time to settle in or finish the meal).
defiReputexa.journeyStep1MsgStandard: ‘Hello [First Name]! It’s the team from [Establishment]. Our goal is mainly to improve: can we count on two little minutes from you to tell us what you liked or what we could do better after your visit? Just respond yes or no as it’s easiest for you.’
defiReputexa.journeyStep1MsgChallenge: Same opening message; the reminder 'little team competition' comes at the next step, only if the client said yes.
defiReputexa.journeyStep1BranchNo: If no: polite closure message, no further follow-up for this collection, and the contact is blacklisted for this collection (no recontact for this programme, according to your setting).
defiReputexa.journeyStep1Stop: At any time: STOP → same closure, blacklist for this collection, immediate stop.
defiReputexa.journeyStep2Context: The AI knows the context (e.g., lunch formula + coffee). The client responds in text or voice.
defiReputexa.journeyStep2MsgStandard: ‘Great, thank you! In a few words, how was your [product/service]? What did you like, and is there a detail we could do better for you?’
defiReputexa.journeyStep2MsgChallenge: ‘Great, thank you! In a few words, how was your [product/service]? Did a team member make your moment special? This month we’re playing a little game internally for excellence 🏆: if you wish, you can mention a name and say what you liked or what could have gone better.’
defiReputexa.journeyStep2Footer: Your personalised text (field 'Challenge') can complement this tone; remain optional and light.
defiReputexa.journeyStep3Example: The client writes e.g.: ‘It was great, very good coffee and the blonde waitress was super quick.’
defiReputexa.journeyStep3Ai: The AI proposes a ready-to-copy version: same words, nothing invented, discreet local SEO. The client has the final say: they can request as many edits as they want — text, voice, or photos — until full validation.
defiReputexa.journeyStep3ChallengeNote: With the challenge, we naturally encourage keeping the mention of the person cited if the client has already written it.
defiReputexa.journeyStep4b: Button like ‘Publish my review in 2 clicks’ to Google (or other).
defiReputexa.journeyStep5Msg: ‘A huge thank you [First Name]! … As promised on the counter poster, your number will no longer be used for this process… archiving/deletion within 120 days… See you soon at [Establishment]! 👋’
defiReputexa.journeyRecapBody: The final ranking, the reminder of the rewards you entered, and the archivable history: everything is grouped on this page for your team and the public link (team).

reviewBoost.title: A Google Review for REPUTEXA
reviewBoost.description: As simple as a WhatsApp request: you write, the AI can refine the text, then you publish on Google. The stars are chosen on Google, not here. Once finished, this block will not reappear.
reviewBoost.step1: Write a few words about your experience with REPUTEXA.
reviewBoost.step2: Optional: tap ‘Optimise with AI’ for a ready-to-copy text.
reviewBoost.step3: ‘Publish on Google’ saves your testimony, copies the text to the clipboard, and opens the review page in a new tab — choose the stars, paste, send.

reviewCollection.pageSubtitle: Automate WhatsApp review requests after each sale — Zenith Plan.
reviewCollection.paywallBody: Automatic review collection via WhatsApp (POS connection, courtesy window, GDPR) is exclusively available with the Zenith Plan.
reviewCollection.section1Intro: Define the ideal delay before a message is sent to your clients after their visit.
reviewCollection.activityProfileExampleCaption: Typical send window for this profile:
reviewCollection.physicalDelayExplainer: Usually the message goes out about {delay} after the visit, with slight random variation so it feels natural.
reviewCollection.businessTypeHint: Type of operation recorded with the backup:
reviewCollection.ecommerceStrategyIntro: REPUTEXA monitors the tracking number. The countdown only starts when the package is officially delivered (carrier scan).
reviewCollection.ecommerceStrategyImmediate: Immediate pleasure (clothes, gadgets) — send 2h after the ‘Delivered’ scan
reviewCollection.ecommerceStrategyTestMount: Test & assembly (furniture, high-tech) — send 24h after the ‘Delivered’ scan
reviewCollection.ecommerceStrategyCustom: Custom — choose the delay after the ‘Delivered’ scan
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxAfterDelivery: Reviews after delivery: the webhook must send status (e.g. Delivered) and whatsapp_consent: true once the package is delivered.
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxSend2h: REPUTEXA sends the WhatsApp 2h after the ‘Delivered’ scan (carrier).
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxSend24h: REPUTEXA sends the WhatsApp 24h after the ‘Delivered’ scan (carrier).
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxSendCustom: REPUTEXA sends the WhatsApp {delay} after the ‘Delivered’ scan (carrier).
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxShippedOnly: If status = shipped only, nothing is scheduled until it is delivered.
reviewCollection.ecommerceUxRich: <lead>Reviews after delivery</lead>: the webhook must send <mono>status</mono> (e.g. Delivered) and <mono>whatsapp_consent: true</mono> once the package is delivered.
reviewCollection.toastTokenOk: Legacy token regenerated. Update your POS if you are still using this method.
reviewCollection.toastApiKeyOk: Nouvelle clé générée. Mettez à jour votre configuration POS immédiatement.

reviewCollection.rgpdSectionIntro: Pour les boutiques en ligne, le consentement explicite du client avant tout envoi WhatsApp est obligatoire. Utilisez le texte ci-dessous sur votre tunnel de commande (checkout).
reviewCollection.rgpdConsentBody: J'accepte de recevoir une invitation unique par WhatsApp de la part de {establishment} via notre partenaire REPUTEXA pour donner mon avis après la réception de mon colis.
reviewCollection.rgpdHelpTabsLabel: Aide — intégrer le consentement au checkout
reviewCollection.rgpdShopifyLi1: Allez dans votre interface Shopify : <bold>Paramètres > Checkout</bold>.
reviewCollection.rgpdShopifyLi3: Cochez <bold>« Afficher une option d'abonnement au passage à la caisse »</bold>.
reviewCollection.rgpdShopifyLi4: Dans le champ <bold>« Texte d'adhésion »</bold>, collez le texte REPUTEXA (copié ci-dessus).
reviewCollection.rgpdStripeLi1: Dans votre Dashboard Stripe, allez dans <bold>Checkout & Payment Links</bold>.
reviewCollection.rgpdStripeLi4: Nommez le label avec le <bold>texte de consentement REPUTEXA</bold> (copié ci-dessus).
reviewCollection.rgpdClauseHeading: Clause à insérer dans vos CGV / Politique de Confidentialité
reviewCollection.rgpdClauseInstruction: <bold>Instruction :</bold> Copiez la clause ci-dessous et insérez-la dans vos CGV en remplaçant « Article X » par le numéro suivant de votre document.
reviewCollection.rgpdClauseBody: Article [X] – Fidélité, collecte d'avis et satisfaction client : nous utilisons la solution REPUTEXA pour gérer notre programme de fidélité (points, récompenses, messages liés aux avantages) et, le cas échéant, pour solliciter un avis après votre passage ou votre commande. Les données nécessaires (identité, téléphone, contexte d'achat, solde de points le cas échéant) sont traitées conformément à notre politique de confidentialité. REPUTEXA agit comme sous-traitant technique sur nos instructions ; les contenus générés par l'IA sont des suggestions que nous validons avant envoi. Les points n'ont pas de valeur monétaire. Lors d'imports de fichiers clients, nous garantissons en avoir la licéité. Répondre STOP met fin aux messages ; un retrait total peut clôturer le compte fidélité selon notre règlement.
reviewCollection.rgpdNoteBody: L'ajout de cette clause est recommandé pour documenter votre devoir d'information (fidélité et avis). Si vous ne l'insérez pas ou ne l'appliquez pas sur votre site et vos documents, la conformité de votre parcours relève de votre seule responsabilité : REPUTEXA ne saurait être tenue responsable des manquements liés à l'information ou au consentement.
reviewCollection.certificateSectionLabel: Certificat de conformité (preuve « fil de preuve »)
reviewCollection.certificateTip: Document de synthèse pour vos dossiers (clients, assureur ou autorité) : version juridique publiée en vigueur, dernier cycle Guardian, engagement de compte — horodatage REPUTEXA.
reviewCollection.section2IntroPhysical: Donnez ces accès à votre fournisseur de logiciel de caisse pour automatiser l'envoi.
reviewCollection.section2IntroEcommerce: Transmettez ce lien à votre intégrateur ou à votre outil d'automatisation (Zapier, Make, etc.) pour déclencher l'envoi après livraison.
reviewCollection.webhookRotateWarning: ⚠️ Rotating the key revokes the old URL — update your POS or Zapier immediately.
reviewCollection.noApiKey: Aucune clé API générée pour ce compte.
reviewCollection.rotateKeyTitle: Générer une nouvelle clé (révoque l'ancienne URL immédiatement)
reviewCollection.zapierHint: Votre caisse ne supporte pas les webhooks directs ?
reviewCollection.posterTip: Conseil : A4 au comptoir ; A5 si peu de place ; A3 pour une affiche murale. Impression à 100 %, sans « Ajuster à la page ».
reviewCollection.ecommerceCheckoutNote: Pour une boutique en ligne, l'insertion effective de la clause (section 1) et son application concrète dans votre tunnel de commande / checkout relèvent de votre responsabilité. Sans cette mise en œuvre sur votre site, REPUTEXA ne saurait être tenu responsable des manquements éventuels à votre devoir d'information ou au recueil du consentement.
reviewCollection.payloadNoAuthNote: Aucun header d'authentification requis — la clé est intégrée dans l'URL.
reviewCollection.ecomCardIdentityBody: first_name et phone — obligatoires pour l'envoi WhatsApp.
reviewCollection.ecomCardComplianceBody: whatsapp_consent doit être true pour autoriser l'envoi.
reviewCollection.ecomCardLogisticsBody: status déclenche l'envoi dès que le colis est marqué livré (ex. Livré, Delivered, Zugestellt…) — tracking_number optionnel.
reviewCollection.webhookPhysicalPayloadExplainer: For brick-and-mortar, send this JSON from your POS via webhook: first name, phone, and optionally a purchase label to personalise the WhatsApp invite. Customers can also enrol via your public page (QR) and add their Wallet pass (Apple or Google depending on device).
reviewCollection.statGeneratedSub: Texte sublimé & publié par le client
reviewCollection.auditIntro: Chaque ligne indique la voie d'entrée (webhook clé API ou Zenith), la date de réception, le contexte connu et le statut. Téléphone partiellement masqué ici ; détail complet (ex. IP) dans l'export RGPD (Paramètres → Télécharger mes données).
reviewCollection.auditRetention: Les identifiants de la file sont anonymisés ou effacés au plus tard à 120 jours (statistiques agrégées pouvant être conservées). Une nouvelle sollicitation WhatsApp pour le même numéro n'est pas envoyée avant 120 jours après le début de la précédente campagne ; passé ce délai, un nouvel envoi redevient possible hors opposition (STOP).

zapier.title: Connecter via Zapier
zapier.subtitle: +6 000 logiciels compatibles — sans aucune ligne de code
zapier.step1Body: Sur zapier.com, cliquez sur + Create → Zaps.
zapier.t2a: Cherchez « Webhooks by Zapier » dans les applications
zapier.t2c: Zapier vous génère un lien → copiez-le et collez-le dans votre logiciel comme adresse d'envoi
zapier.t3a: Cherchez à nouveau « Webhooks by Zapier » dans les actions
zapier.t3c: Copiez votre lien REPUTEXA ci-dessous, puis remplissez le tableau champ par champ
zapier.ecoStatus: Statut livraison — l'envoi se programme si Delivered
zapier.ecoQueueNote: Tant que le colis n'est pas livré (Delivered), rien n'est mis en file d'attente.
zapier.wrapRequest: Envelopper la requête dans un tableau
zapier.reassurance: Si vous voyez d'autres options, ignorez-les simplement. Votre configuration est déjà parfaite.
zapier.footerTeam: Notre équipe technique est à votre disposition.

reviewCollection.ecommerceModalTitle: L'envoi de WhatsApp en e-commerce nécessite l'ajout de la clause Article X dans vos CGV et une case à cocher dans votre checkout. Avez-vous bien configuré votre tunnel de commande ?
reviewCollection.ecommerceModalWarning: L'activation de vos campagnes sans ces mentions légales est sous votre entière responsabilité. Si la clause et la case à cocher ne sont pas réellement mises en place sur votre site, REPUTEXA ne saurait être tenue responsable des manquements liés à l'information ou au consentement.
reviewCollection.ecommerceModalCta: Je m'engage à configurer mon checkout
reviewCollection.pageFooterLegal: REPUTEXA est un prestataire technique (sous-traitant). L'usage de ce service implique l'acceptation de nos Conditions Générales d'Utilisation et de notre politique de confidentialité (Fidélité et Avis) ; la collecte du consentement et l'information des clients finaux relèvent du Responsable de traitement (commerçant).

llmBudget.title: Garde-fou consommation IA
llmBudget.subtitle: Compteur du jour (UTC, remise à zéro à minuit UTC)
llmBudget.explanation: Chaque réponse assistée par l’IA, synthèse ou message automatisé compte comme un passage. Tant que vous restez sous le premier seuil, la réactivité reste maximale. Au-delà, le service peut légèrement ralentir pour protéger votre budget (et le nôtre). Au plafond absolu, les nouvelles actions IA sont mises en pause jusqu’au prochain jour UTC — d’où l’intérêt de suivre cette jauge.
llmBudget.statusOk: Vous êtes dans la zone confort — tout est fluide.
llmBudget.statusSoft: Au-delà du seuil confort : des ralentissements discrets peuvent apparaître.
llmBudget.statusHard: Plafond atteint : les actions IA reprendront après minuit UTC.

adminUpdatesForm.scheduleHelp: Par défaut, le communiqué est visible tout de suite pour les utilisateurs connectés. En mode planifié, la date/heure saisie est interprétée dans le fuseau du navigateur (convertie en UTC côté serveur). Bornes : au moins ~30 s dans le futur, max. ~12 mois — comme la diffusion e-mail planifiée, sans lien avec le préavis légal 30 jours.
adminUpdatesForm.subtitle: Visible uniquement par vous · Sublimation IA disponible
adminUpdatesForm.scheduleHelpHtml: Par défaut, le communiqué est visible tout de suite pour les utilisateurs connectés. En mode planifié, l’heure choisie suit le fuseau de ce navigateur (convertie en UTC côté serveur). Bornes : au moins ~30 s dans le futur, max. ~12 mois — comme la diffusion e-mail planifiée, sans lien avec le préavis légal 30 jours.

weeklyInsight.introNote: Les semaines les plus récentes sont en tête de liste.

reviewsPage.pageSubtitle: Gérez vos avis et la file de publication automatique.
reviewsPage.queueSectionDesc: File d’attente : réponses prêtes ou en attente de validation. Délai humain avant envoi sur la plateforme ; vous gardez la main pour modifier, reporter ou annuler.
reviewsPage.publishedDesc: Réponses passées en statut publié (y compris après la file d'attente).
reviewsPage.publishedEmpty: Aucune réponse publiée sur cette période / cet établissement.
reviewsPage.explainerItem1: Les avis importés (Google, etc.) apparaissent ici dès réception.
reviewsPage.explainerItem2: Notes 4 et 5 : une réponse IA est générée puis mise en file de publication automatique après un délai de quelques heures.
reviewsPage.explainerItem3: Notes 1 à 3 — plan Vision : vous validez ou modifiez la suggestion sur cet onglet. Plans Pulse et Zénith : vous recevez en plus une alerte WhatsApp (avis + suggestion) pour réagir depuis votre téléphone.
reviewsPage.explainerItem4: Toutes les réponses passent par une file d’attente (environ 2 h à 7 h) pour un rythme crédible. Si plusieurs avis arrivent en même temps, les envois sont espacés automatiquement (quelques minutes entre chaque).
reviewsPage.explainerItem5: Pendant la file, vous pouvez modifier le texte, reporter la publication (+1 h ou +2 h) ou annuler l’envoi.
reviewsPage.explainerItem6: Après publication, l’avis figure dans la section « Publiés ».
reviewsPage.searchReviewsPlaceholder: Rechercher par nom du client ou extrait d’avis…

alertsPage.legalComplaintTemplate:
Objet : Demande de suppression d'avis non conforme

Madame, Monsieur,

Nous avons constaté la présence sur votre plateforme d'un avis publié par {reviewerName}, qui ne respecte pas vos conditions d'utilisation en vigueur.

Cet avis contient des propos haineux, diffamatoires et/ou manifestement faux, portant gravement atteinte à notre réputation professionnelle et à l'intégrité de notre établissement.

En vertu de votre politique de contenu et du droit applicable, nous vous demandons de procéder à la suppression immédiate de cet avis dans les meilleurs délais.

Nous restons à votre disposition pour tout renseignement complémentaire.

Cordialement,
L'équipe REPUTEXA Shield

bananoWalletDesigner.panelLead: Near full-size preview (iPhone-style): colours, images, and strip mood. Three colours and images go into the .pkpass; preview-only CSS is web-only.
bananoWalletDesigner.sectionInspirationLead: Full-bleed SMB themes — one click applies palette, illustration, strip, and light; fine‑tune, then save.
bananoWalletDesigner.themeIllustrationHint: A vector illustration fills the whole card behind the UI; blur and gradients keep titles and points readable.
bananoWalletDesigner.sectionAiLead: Describe your business (sector, style, colours). The AI picks the trade illustration, colours, and preview CSS (no AI-generated bitmaps — bundled SVG backgrounds).
bananoWalletDesigner.aiPromptPlaceholder: e.g. cosy artisan bakery, warm golden tones… or wine bar, burgundy and cream…
bananoWalletDesigner.aiUnavailable: AI assistant unavailable (OpenAI not configured on the server).
bananoWalletDesigner.aiEmptyPrompt: Describe what you want before running the AI.
bananoWalletDesigner.fieldLogoHint: PNG or JPG — aim for at least 480×480 px so the logo stays crisp on the pass.
bananoWalletDesigner.fieldStripHint: Landscape like Apple Wallet (~3:1), e.g. 1125×369 or 750×246 px — fills the area above the points.
bananoWalletDesigner.stripCropLead: Focale horizontale et verticale, zoom. Même logique que la génération du fichier Apple Wallet.
bananoWalletDesigner.fieldLogoTextPlaceholder: Shown at the top of the pass
bananoWalletDesigner.hintCustomCss: Injected only in this preview (not in the .pkpass export). Useful selectors: .wallet-pass-preview-card, .wallet-pass-preview-strip, .wallet-pass-preview-ambient.
bananoWalletDesigner.previewBackSubtitle: Champs tels qu’affichés dans Wallet (lien, historique).
bananoWalletDesigner.previewDetailSubtitle: Après ouverture du passe dans Wallet
bananoWalletDesigner.previewDetailQrCaption: QR à présenter en caisse pour identifier la carte.

guidelines.idealFormat: Ideal size: 1125 x 369 px (about a 3:1 ratio).
guidelines.qualityHint: Use an HD illustration for a sharp Retina look on iPhone.
guidelines.safeZone: Safe area: the shop name may sit over the strip, so keep the main subject (for example the butcher) centred or toward the right.
guidelines.stripTooNarrow: This image is a bit small and may look soft on newer phones.
guidelines.templatesLead: Pick a look for the preview and colours. HD strip art for the Apple pass will be available here soon.
guidelines.templatesFootnote: These apply the trade theme and colours; a premium strip illustration library will follow.

bananoWalletDesigner.sectionPresetRailLead: One click applies colours, strip banner and illustration. Save to confirm.
bananoWalletDesigner.sectionStampIconsLead: Preview of stamp slots on the card (stamp loyalty).
bananoWalletDesigner.designAccentTip: The #BF174C accent highlights the active selection in this panel.

bananoParametres.automationSaveSuccessLead: Your automations are live. Your loyalty engine is ready.
bananoParametres.tagBarAria: Insert a variable into the message
bananoParametres.testModeLead: Get a real WhatsApp on your account number (phone or WhatsApp from Account settings) to check formatting.
bananoParametres.testSendVip: Send me the "VIP of the month" sample
bananoParametres.testSentToast: Sample WhatsApp sent. Check your phone.
bananoParametres.loadLoyaltyFailed: Could not load the loyalty program.
bananoParametres.loadRulesFailed: Could not load automation rules (Supabase migration / pilot tables).
bananoParametres.headerLead: Program settings (points or stamp threshold, reward) and automatic WhatsApp follow-ups (inactive customer, birthday, VIP of the month). The POS terminal uses these values live.
bananoParametres.relancesTabsLead: Four scenarios in the tabs below: win-back (inactive clients), new member welcome, VIP of the month, and birthdays.
bananoParametres.segmentRecoveryLead: Clients inactifs — ton humain, écoute, vouvoiement.
bananoParametres.segmentWelcomeLead: Nouveaux membres — message le lendemain de l’inscription (réglable), accueil chaleureux.
bananoParametres.segmentPrivilegeLead: Meilleur client du mois civil précédent (CA ou passages).
bananoParametres.segmentBirthdayLead: Anniversaire : message jour J et rappel avant (paramétrable).
bananoParametres.whatsappHelpA: Unchecked = no automatic send for this rule (the cron skips the row). You only write the personal middle line. The first name comes from the customer record, and the intro uses the business name saved for your store:
bananoParametres.whatsappHelpC: . Also set birthday dates on records (Customer base). VIP of the month uses the previous calendar month; automatic send on the 1st (Europe/Paris) when the rule is on. Delivery: cron + Twilio.
bananoParametres.establishmentNameFallback: not set — “our team” will be used when sending
bananoParametres.newClientDisabled: Désactivé : aucun message d’accueil automatique après inscription. Cochez et enregistrez pour réactiver.
bananoParametres.newClientDelayLabel: Envoyer combien de jours après l’inscription (heure Paris)
bananoParametres.placeholderWelcome: Ex. : avantages Pass activés, remerciement avant le cadeau de bienvenue…
bananoParametres.newClientDiscountTitle: Cadeau de bienvenue dans le message
bananoParametres.lostClientDisabled: Off: no automatic “lost customer” follow-up. Check the box and save to re-enable.
bananoParametres.daysInactiveLabel: Days without a visit before sending
bananoParametres.lostInactiveTradeRecommendedAria: Apply the recommendation for your trade (onboarding industry): {days} days without a visit
bananoParametres.placeholderLost: E.g. ask how they are, offer to listen without pressure, invite them back — always address them as “you” formally in your language.
bananoParametres.birthdayLabel: Birthday (exact day, date on customer record)
bananoParametres.birthdayDisabled: Off: no automatic message on the day. Check and save to re-enable (birthday stays on the record).
bananoParametres.birthdayAnticipationHint: A few days ahead, a message reminds them to visit, bring ID, and use the Wallet pass. The gift voucher and main message go out on the morning of the birthday.
bananoParametres.placeholderBirth: E.g. the whole team wishes you a happy birthday…
bananoParametres.vipLabel: VIP of the month (highest revenue in the past calendar month)

bananoVoucherArchive.emptyArchive: Aucune archive pour l'instant. Le premier export CSV est créé automatiquement au prochain passage du cron (1er du mois vers 8h UTC, comme le rapport performance IA), ou contactez le support pour une extraction manuelle urgente.

bananoPublicJoin.successCard: Carte créée. Ajoutez-la à votre Wallet pour présenter le code en caisse.
bananoPublicJoin.subtitle: Fidélité — cadeau de bienvenue en quelques secondes.

bananoManualWhatsapp.title: Message WhatsApp manuel
bananoManualWhatsapp.scheduleHint: Envoi via Twilio (hors scénarios Zenith automatisés). Respectez l'opposition client (STOP). Vous restez responsable du contenu et du respect du RGPD.
bananoManualWhatsapp.toastPickSchedule: Choisissez une date et heure d'envoi.
bananoManualWhatsapp.toastScheduleLead: L'envoi programmé doit être au moins dans environ 2 minutes.

bananoGhostTokens.title: Terminal & Agents — Sentinel Ghost
bananoGhostTokens.intro: Chaque PC caisse Windows reçoit un jeton secret (Bearer). Collez-le dans l’Agent Ghost pour résoudre les scans REP-… (clients et collaborateurs avec crédit staff), les bons VCHR-… et les transacts. Le secret n’est montré qu’une seule fois.
bananoGhostTokens.testMacroRest: uniquement sur le PC Windows — dans l’Agent Ghost, renseignez un montant fictif puis « Tester SendInput » pour voir comment votre logiciel réagit aux touches injectées (sans toucher à la fidélité en base).
bananoGhostTokens.empty: Aucun agent. Créez un jeton pour connecter le poste caisse Windows.
bananoGhostTokens.dialogHint: Après validation, un jeton secret s’affichera une seule fois. Copiez-le dans l’application Reputexa Ghost (champ jeton Bearer).
bananoGhostTokens.secretBody: Pour votre sécurité, il ne sera plus jamais affiché. Seul un hash est conservé en base ; en cas de perte, révoquez l’accès et créez un nouvel agent.
bananoGhostTokens.toastCreateOk: Agent créé — copiez le jeton maintenant
bananoGhostTokens.confirmRevoke: Révoquer l’accès « {label} » ? L’application Windows ne pourra plus appeler l’API avec ce jeton.

bananoComptaExpert.lockedBody: Ce module (synthèse mensuelle fidélité + collaborateurs, exports PDF et CSV pour votre équipe comptable) est inclus dans le plan {plan}. Vous tracez chaque bon avec date, bénéficiaire et montant : fini les écarts « invisibles » entre caisse et avantages offerts.
bananoComptaExpert.title: Expert-Comptable — pièces justificatives
bananoComptaExpert.intro: Total des remises fidélité (bons utilisés) et des avantages collaborateurs réellement consommés en caisse. À transmettre au comptable avec vos journaux : même principe qu’un rapport d’opérations marketing ou d’avantages en nature tracés. Méthode pro recommandée : créer un mode de paiement ou une ligne de remise « REPUTEXA » sur votre logiciel de caisse pour un rapprochement sans friction.
bananoComptaExpert.loyaltySub: Cumul bons à montant fixe (€). Hors pourcentage : {percentCount} bon(s) % · libellé seul : {labelOnlyCount}. Utilisations totales : {redemptions}.
bananoComptaExpert.staffSub: Somme des débits enregistrés en caisse sur la période. Mouvements : {debitEventCount}.
bananoComptaExpert.footnote: Note : ce document sert de pièce justificative de synthèse pour les remises marketing fidélité et les avantages en nature collaborateurs enregistrés via REPUTEXA ; il complète vos justificatifs de caisse sans remplacer vos obligations légales ou déclaratives.

bananoSentinel.explainerBody: It is the live "Ghost" event feed: loyalty card scans, enrolments, credits or debits through the Windows agent or integrations, voucher redemptions, device links. Use it to check in real time that pass-throughs and the Wallet behave correctly. It is not the hourly cash flow chart; that one is under Analytics - Cash flow.
bananoSentinel.title: Sentinel Live Feed
bananoSentinel.subtitle: Flux temps réel des scans et transacts Agent Ghost (Supabase Realtime).
bananoSentinel.empty: Aucun événement Ghost pour l’instant. Les scans depuis l’agent Windows apparaîtront ici.

bananoTerminal.terminalFsUniqueBody: L'URL contient un code propre à votre enseigne (après /terminal/). Plusieurs caissiers : aucun souci. Deux enseignes REPUTEXA : jamais le même lien.
bananoTerminal.terminalFsConv2: Majuscules, sans accents ; apostrophe → espace (O BRIEN LIAM).
bananoTerminal.terminalFsConv3: À l'enregistrement : normalisation auto — vérifiez avant validation.
bananoTerminal.terminalDashUniqueBody: L'URL du terminal contient un code propre à votre enseigne (après /terminal/). Plusieurs caissiers ou une même tablette en magasin : aucun souci. En revanche, deux clients REPUTEXA différents n'utilisent jamais le même lien.
bananoTerminal.terminalDashConv1: Toujours nom de famille d’abord, puis prénom (ex. LEFEVRE ADRIEN).
bananoTerminal.terminalDashConv2: Majuscules, sans accents, pas d’apostrophe (remplacer par un espace : O BRIEN LIAM).
bananoTerminal.terminalDashConv3: À l’enregistrement : normalisation automatique (accents, apostrophes, casse). Vérifiez avant validation.
